subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Drainpipe Water Flow — Cubic Feet In/Out, Min Water Time & Overflow Equation
stimulus: The rate at which rainwater flows into a drainpipe is modeled by the function $R$, where $R(t) = 20\sin\left(\frac{t^2}{35}\right)$ cubic feet per hour, $t$ is measured in hours, and $0 \le t \le 8$. The pipe is partially blocked, allowing water to drain out the other end of the pipe at a rate modeled by $D(t) = -0.04t^3 + 0.4t^2 + 0.96t$ cubic feet per hour, for $0 \le t \le 8$. There are 30 cubic feet of water in the pipe at time $t = 0$.

parts.prompt: How many cubic feet of rainwater flow into the pipe during the 8-hour time interval $0 \le t \le 8$?
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $\int_0^8 R(t)\, dt \approx 76.570$ cubic feet of rainwater flow into the pipe.

parts.prompt: Is the amount of water in the pipe increasing or decreasing at time $t = 3$ hours? Give a reason for your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $R(3) - D(3) \approx -0.314 < 0$. Since $R(3) < D(3)$, the amount of water is decreasing at $t = 3$.

scoring_points.description: Correct answer with reason: the amount of water is decreasing at $t = 3$ because $R(3) < D(3)$ (the rate draining out exceeds the rate flowing in)

required_elements: Correct answer with reason: the amount of water is decreasing at $t = 3$ because $R(3) < D(3)$ (the rate draining out exceeds the rate flowing in)

parts.prompt: At what time $t$, $0 \le t \le 8$, is the amount of water in the pipe at a minimum? Justify your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Amount of water $= 30 + \int_0^t [R(x)-D(x)]\, dx$. Critical points: $t \approx 3.272$. Values: $30$ (at $t=0$), $\approx 27.965$ (at $t \approx 3.272$), $\approx 48.544$ (at $t=8$). Minimum at $t \approx 3.272$.

scoring_points.description: Sets $R(t) - D(t) = 0$ to find critical points

required_elements: Sets $R(t) - D(t) = 0$ to find critical points

scoring_points.description: Justification: evaluates amount of water at $t = 0$, $t \approx 3.272$, and $t = 8$; values are $30$, $\approx 27.965$, $\approx 48.544$; minimum is $\approx 27.965$ at $t \approx 3.272$

required_elements: Justification: evaluates amount of water at $t = 0$, $t \approx 3.272$,

parts.prompt: The pipe can hold 50 cubic feet of water before overflowing. For $t > 8$, water continues to flow into and out of the pipe at the given rates until the pipe begins to overflow. Write, but do not solve, an equation involving one or more integrals that gives the time $w$ when the pipe will begin to overflow.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $30 + \int_0^w [R(t) - D(t)]\, dt = 50$ (do not solve for $w$).
